Off Calendar Mar-23-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jessica Taran, Esq., TARAN LAW, 917)686-0195, jtaran@jtaranlaw.com, for Plaintiff Alex Miretsky
Lauren Quan, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF ANDREW QUAN, (510) 394-2466, lauren@quan.legal for Defendant HANI HADDAD

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argumentadopts the tentative ruling as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT HANI HADDAD'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff Alex Miretskys Corrected First Amended Verified Complaint.

Defendant's Motion to Strike as to the claim for prejudgment interest is denied. Defendant moves to strike Prayer for Relief, paragraph 2 in its entirety: "an award of interest accrued as of the date of the judgment at the rate of three percent (3%) per month; ". (CFAVC, Prayer for Relief 2.) "The test for determining certainty under section 3287(a) is whether the defendant knew the amount of damages owed to the claimant [?] The certainty required by section 3287(a) is not lost when the existence of liability turns on disputed facts but only when the amount of damages turns on disputed facts. (Olson v. Cory (1983) 35 Cal.3d 390, 402.) Here, the damages are certain. (CRAVC 30; See CCP sec. 3287(a) [prejudgment interest recoverable where damages are certain].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.
